title: conyza subspicata
intro: Conyza subspicata, also known as Horseweeds or Horseweed, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Conyza subspicata include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a diuretic to help reduce fever and relieve menstrual cramps. Conyza subspicata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
